name: Muslim American Youth Foundation
mission: Our mission is to educate and instil a drive for personal academic success, enrich through experience and expansion of what is really possible, and enlighten by means of taking the aforementioned experiences and incorporating them into their personal lives, households and communities.
theoryOfChange: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) outlines its strategic approach under the 'Our Strategy' section on its 'About Us' page and further elaborates on its programs. This framework details how their programs in learning, athletics, and fostering success are designed to help youth develop a strong sense of personal, academic, and spiritual identity, and equip them with skills and knowledge to overcome challenges. Their purpose is further described through initiatives in outreach, advocacy, education, youth empowerment, resource dissemination, and community service, all aimed at fostering personal growth and providing holistic support to Muslim-American youth.
scoreSummary: Muslim American Youth Foundation shows strong impact and good alignment.

evaluationSources: Healthy Environment for All (HEAL) Capacity Grant (Washington State Department of Health (DOH))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

zakat.claim_evidence: The Muslim American Youth Foundation's donation page and mission statement do not explicitly mention 'zakat' or dedicated zakat funds. While an affiliated organization, the Islamic Association of North America (IANA), has a 'Zakat Calculation' link, the prompt specifies that international affiliates or parent organizations are different entities and evidence must be from the specific US organization being evaluated. (Source: https://www.mayf.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: The organization holds 13.4 months of working capital and is categorized under youth and community services.
The foundation reported total revenue of $2,087,958 and a program expense ratio of 72.1%.
Programs include community services, youth sports, athletics, and mentoring.
The organization accepts Sadaqah donations to support its facility and youth programs.
The foundation provides engagement opportunities for community members to support youth enlightenment.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its solid financial stability and efficient fundraising, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building rather than direct service metrics.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance transparency regarding specific program outcomes and impact metrics
Increase program expense ratio to align more closely with high-performing benchmarks

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Sadaqah-eligible entity, the organization offers a medium fit for Muslim donors looking to support domestic poverty and youth development [4]. The cause is considered urgent within the context of domestic community support, though it does not currently facilitate zakat-specific giving [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation allocates a program expense ratio of 72.1% toward its core mission of youth education and community services [2]. While it maintains a healthy 13.4 months of working capital, the impact is categorized as capacity building, reflecting a focus on infrastructure and facility operations [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Youth Foundation provides a dedicated facility for the education, enrichment, and enlightenment of youth through comprehensive community and athletic programming.
strengths: Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
High liquidity with 13.4 months of working capital available
Dedicated focus on domestic youth enrichment and sports mentoring
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in the United States, the Muslim American Youth Foundation operates as a Sadaqah-eligible organization focused on youth development and community services [1][4]. The foundation reported a total revenue of $2,087,958, which supports its mission of creating a safe environment for mentoring and athletics [2][3].

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's high liquidity and rapid growth suggest it is in a scaling phase where infrastructure investment may temporarily depress program ratios.
risk_factors: <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 72.1%</cite> is lower than many peer organizations.
<cite id="26">Lack of specific beneficiary data</cite> prevents rigorous cost-effectiveness evaluation.
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="5">lower-than-average program spending</cite> and <cite id="25">CEO compensation</cite> which, at $91,000, warrants scrutiny for an organization of this revenue size.

data_gaps: Not fully rated by Charity Navigator
confidence.notes: Financial data is robust, but impact transparency is limited.

alignment.explanation: The charity aligns with Muslim donors through its <cite id="22">Islamic identity</cite> and focus on <cite id="23">fi sabilillah (in the way of Allah)</cite> through youth enrichment.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="24">clearly stating its sadaqah-only status</cite> on a dedicated giving page to avoid donor confusion.

credibility.explanation: Donors can verify the organization's standing through its <cite id="11">100% independent board</cite> and <cite id="18">detailed Form 990 filings</cite>. The organization also maintains <cite id="19">documented theory of change</cite> materials.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by obtaining a full rating from Charity Navigator and publishing audited financial statements.

impact.explanation: The foundation demonstrates impact through <cite id="20">direct capacity building</cite> and a <cite id="21">verified evidence and outcomes</cite> framework. However, the <cite id="5">72.1% program ratio</cite> suggests room for more direct spending on services.
impact.improvement: To strengthen impact, the charity should <cite id="14">publish beneficiary counts consistently</cite> and document triggers for the deployment of its high reserves.

rich_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Youth Foundation provides a dedicated facility for the education, enrichment, and enlightenment of youth through comprehensive community and athletic programming.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize domestic youth development and want to support a rapidly growing, fiscally lean organization focused on safe community spaces.
donor_motivations: Value local community infrastructure and athletic mentoring
Prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead
Want to support the holistic development of Muslim American youth
Are comfortable with sadaqah-only giving for long-term capacity building
giving_considerations: Donations support facility operations and staff mentoring
The organization is in a high-growth phase with significant cash reserves
Impact is measured through enrichment quality rather than high-volume relief
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those looking for international emergency relief operations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a verified theory of change and documented outcomes, though it lacks public cost-per-beneficiary metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By providing a dedicated facility for sports, mentoring, and faith programs, the foundation creates a protective environment that fosters youth enlightenment and community health.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that youth programs actually lead to the intended enrichment and enlightenment outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 73.3% growth rate and expanding program areas like health advocacy, the foundation has significant capacity to absorb new capital for facility expansion.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="6">exceptionally low fundraising cost of $0.03 per $1</cite> and a <cite id="4">73.3% revenue growth rate</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="6">$0.03 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, ensuring that donor acquisition costs remain remarkably low.
MAYF maintains <cite id="10">13.4 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a significant buffer for long-term sustainability.
The foundation has achieved a <cite id="4">73.3% three-year revenue CAGR</cite>, indicating rapidly expanding community support and scale.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim American Youth Foundation exhibits strong organizational health through its governance and financial management. The organization operates with an <cite id="11">independent board of directors (100%)</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of its mission to create facilities for youth enrichment. Financially, the organization has grown its <cite id="12">net assets to $1,699,829</cite> as of 2023, up from $1,204,183 in 2021. This growth is supported by a diversified revenue stream that includes <cite id="13">$225,128 in program service revenue</cite> alongside traditional contributions, suggesting a sustainable model for its athletic and mentoring facilities.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) is a domestic non-profit organization dedicated to the <cite id="1">education, enrichment, and enlightenment of youth</cite> through a comprehensive suite of community and athletic programming. Operating primarily within the United States, the foundation provides a safe environment for <cite id="2">mentoring, youth sports, and faith-based programs</cite> designed to foster healthy development. In the 2023 tax year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,734,105</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 73.3%</cite>. While the organization maintains a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 72.1%</cite>, it demonstrates exceptional fiscal discipline in its development efforts, maintaining a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond athletics, MAYF facilitates <cite id="7">health advocacy and access to services</cite>, ensuring a holistic approach to community welfare. The organization also acts as a bridge for broader humanitarian efforts, as evidenced by <cite id="8">$174,234 in total grants</cite> distributed to domestic partners, including <cite id="9">$81,803 to the Humanitarian African Relief Organization</cite>.
